slides.title: Introduction to K-Means
slides.content: K-Means is a popular clustering algorithm known for its speed and scalability. It involves partitioning data into K distinct clusters based on feature similarity.

slides.title: How K-Means Works
slides.content: The algorithm iteratively assigns data points to clusters and adjusts the cluster centroids. The goal is to minimize the variance within each cluster and achieve convergence.

slides.title: Mathematical Representation
slides.content: K-Means aims to minimize the sum of squared distances between data points and their respective cluster centroids.

slides.title: Choosing the Right K
slides.content: Selecting the number of clusters (K) is crucial. It can be determined using methods like the Elbow Method, which evaluates the sum of squared errors for different K values.

slides.title: Practical Applications
slides.content: K-Means is widely used in market segmentation, image compression, and pattern recognition. It helps in identifying natural groupings in data.

slides.title: Python Code Example
slides.content: Here's a basic implementation of K-Means clustering using Python's scikit-learn library.

visual_assets.code:
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=3)
kmeans.fit(data)

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: K-Means is an efficient clustering technique suitable for large datasets. However, it requires careful selection of K and initial centroids to achieve optimal results.
